text.content:
Chapter 5 — Skeletons of Scarwall. Across Belkzen to the drowned castle where Kazavon died, for the one blade that can unmake the Crown: Serithtial. PCs begin at L13; retrieve the sword and stand at least well into L15 before Chapter 6.

The Fifth Harrowing opens the chapter (suit of keys — dexterity, locks, escapes). The Devil You Know: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.shadowcountSial1]{Shadowcount Sial} and his casket-eidolon Asyra formally join (with @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.laoriVaus0000001]{Laori}): the Brotherhood knows the castle's history and wants Kazavon never to rise — an alliance of genuine use and genuinely awful long-term implications (see the Star Tower). The PCs travel via the Shoanti and the World's Edge passes; the castle stands in a crater lake (the Tarnworm, a L13 lake-serpent, takes boats; the Air Approach answers to @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.aerilayaBanshe01]{Aerilaya}).

The Curse (castle-wide, CL 20th — read this page before running anything):

  • Aura of Menace: detect-style divinations of evil/undead fail: @Check[type:will|dc:25] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}. The whole castle reads as one necromantic aura.
  • Dread: sleeping inside: @Check[type:will|dc:20] on waking or the rest grants nothing — @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued}, no spell recovery (mental, fear).
  • Soultrapping: a living creature dying inside: @Check[type:will|dc:23] or its soul binds into the walls (resurrection requires dispelling that bind and beginning the raise within 1 round). Any undead created inside is bound instantly; a week's residence forces @Check[type:will|dc:22] weekly.
  • Unhallow + dimensional lock: non-inhabitants cannot teleport within or out; vitality channels at −4, void at +4.
  • Phantasms: one-shot terrors per location — flavor, no effect. Self-repair: the castle heals over a year.

SPIRITUAL DOOM — Zellara: if her deck crosses the causeway, Mithrodar attacks her spirit within 2 rounds — @Check[type:will|dc:25] each round (the bearer may substitute their own save); failure = absorbed, the deck dies, and only lifting the curse frees her. Leave the deck off the island. Possession-style magic: @Check[type:will|dc:15] on casting or soul-bound (@Check[type:arcana|dc:25] while casting notices in time to abort). Speak with dead auto-fails (@Check[type:fortitude|dc:20] or permanently @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.9PR9y0bi4JPKnHPR]{Deafened}).

BREAKING IT (@Check[type:arcana|dc:25] to learn remove curse won't): destroy the four spirit anchors — @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.belshallamUmbr01]{Belshallam} (B23), @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.castothraneSk001]{Castothrane} (D1), @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.nihilDevil000001]{Nihil} (D14), @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.zevRavenkaDemi01]{Zev Ravenka} (F11) — then @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mithrodarChain01]{Mithrodar} (C14). Each anchor down peels a layer (his sheet has the ladder) — and the castle screams. He can chain replacements (never constructs, never Ildervok; Sial and Laori qualify). All four down: the locks fall, and he can die. His death frees every soul — and wakes Serithtial.

The castle's society of horrors — play them against each other:

  • Anchors: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.belshallamUmbr01]{Belshallam} (umbral dragon, B23) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.castothraneSk001]{Castothrane} (gatehouse captain, D1) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.nihilDevil000001]{Nihil} (chained devil-assassin, D14 — wants out) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.zevRavenkaDemi01]{Zev Ravenka} (demilich, F11).
  • The keep: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mithrodarChain01]{Mithrodar} (C14) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lashtonDreadSk01]{Lashton} (causeway) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mandraivusLost01]{Mandraivus the Lost} (B5) · the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.danseMacabre0001]{Danse Macabre} (B36 — feared by everyone).
  • The wings: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ukwarFext0000001]{Ukwar} (fext, guest wing) · Anizora (debilitated @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.IrssIkWkW6fsbHJL]{dybbuk}, the smithy floor) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.aerilayaBanshe01]{Aerilaya} (banshee, the cupola) · Glimkarus (a bogeyman tourist who thinks staying is his idea).
  • The towers: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gorstavJuju00001]{Gorstav} (War Tower) · Risibeth (sayona — blood for secrets: names three anchors, points at the chapel) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.prelateAruth0001]{Prelate Aruth} + @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.zevRavenkaDemi01]{Zev} (donjon) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ildervokNight001]{Ildervok} (Star Tower, Zon-Kuthon's writ) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kleestadPharma01]{Kleestad} (the lake below).

Random encounters (1 in 6 per hour moving, flavored per wing): @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.scarwallGuard001]{Scarwall guards}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Gu0cJHGwPd547OtC]{wraiths}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.5meW7DytYnF7Iq2V]{greater shadows}, @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gargoyleBrute001]{gargoyle packs}, wall-spirits — the spirits stop roaming at 3 anchors down.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.5, A — Barbican — p.262.

The causeway runs to a squat barbican whose arrow-slits glow with orc-fire — the only living things for forty miles, and proud of it.

The Deadwatchers hold the gate: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.deadwatcherOrc01]{orc warriors} at the entry (A2, 4×) and barracks (A6, 8×), watch-posts (A11), and @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urySevenskulls01]{Ury Sevenskulls} in his chamber (A10). Fight or talk — @Check[type:intimidation|dc:28] / @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:32] / a duel or worthy gift buys passage (they will not enter the castle).

  • 8× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.deadwatcherOrc01]{Deadwatcher (7)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urySevenskulls01]{Ury Sevenskulls (10)}
Barbican in armsTrivial · 15 XP @ L13

The full garrison if roused: 8 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.deadwatcherOrc01]{Deadwatchers} + @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urySevenskulls01]{Ury}. The lake routes instead: the Tarnworm (Trivial · 40 XP @ L13, a Huge lake-serpent — use @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kleestadPharma01]{Kleestad's} Strikes at −2) under the boats, or @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.aerilayaBanshe01]{Aerilaya} + gargoyles aloft.

pages.name: B1-B6. First Floor — the Gate & the Hero

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.5, B1-B6 — p.270.

B1, the Causeway: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lashtonDreadSk01]{Lashton} on his @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.skelNightmare001]{skeletal nightmare} with 12 skeletal champions (Trivial · 15 XP @ L13 — the champions are chaff; the welcome is the point). B3: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.scarwallGuard001]{guard} posts (2/room). B4, the Death Chamber: the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.corpseOrgy000001]{Corpse Orgy} (Trivial · 40 XP @ L13). B6: trench-mist corridors (concealment) prowled by 6 juju zombies (Elite @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.WioQ6rOeMRuTOliY]{zombie brutes}).

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mandraivusLost01]{Mandraivus (13)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.vortexMadness001]{Vortex of Madness (10)}
B5 — Mandraivus's FallTrivial · 40 XP @ L13

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mandraivusLost01]{Mandraivus the Lost} beside the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.vortexMadness001]{Vortex of Madness} — fight a hero, or free one (his entry). The room of the chapter's soul.

pages.name: B7-B24. First Floor — Barracks, Kitchens & the Dragon

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.5, B7-B24 — p.276.

  • B7–B14 — The garrison wing: quarters, offices, the magus's and captain's rooms.
  • B10 — Knurlott: the graveknight (link @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.EH61wr2iEKooAzgQ]{Graveknight Warmaster}, renamed — Low · 60 XP @ L13).
  • B15 — The Kitchens: the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.crematoryBlast01]{Crematory Blast} + 4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinderGhoul00001]{cinder ghouls} (Trivial · 40 XP @ L13).
  • B17: 5 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.scarwallGuard001]{Scarwall guards}. B18: the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.polongBottles001]{Polong Bottles}. B20: the wine cellar.
  • B19 — Malatrothe: a hollow caretaker-wight (run as an Elite @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.ZQ7lS9TYOFv07m1a]{war-wraith}).
  • B21 — The Courtyard: a corpse lotus (Trivial · 40 XP @ L13) — Huge carrion-plant; vines reach 20 ft, @Check[type:reflex|dc:33] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} and fed toward the maw (@Damage[3d10+11[bludgeoning]] per round).
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.belshallamUmbr01]{Belshallam (15) — ANCHOR}
B23 — Belshallam's LairModerate · 80 XP @ L13

The @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.belshallamUmbr01]{umbral dragon} — SPIRIT ANCHOR — among his worm-pets (B22: 4 tenebrous worms, L9 shadow-larvae: bristles @Check[type:fortitude|dc:28] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} 1 round). His hoard (B24): the wing's treasury, re-scaled L13–14 parcel + a shadow rune and the castle's best mundane plunder.

pages.name: B25-B37. The Guest Wing & the Observatory

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.5, B25-B37 — p.284.

B26: Pegg & Loute, two L6 'entertainers' juggling cleavers for a dead audience. B29: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ukwarFext0000001]{Ukwar} holds court (Trivial · 30 XP @ L13). B30/B32: haunted baths (festering spirits — 6× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.5meW7DytYnF7Iq2V]{greater shadows}, Trivial · 0 XP @ L13). B33: wraith-guests (10× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Gu0cJHGwPd547OtC]{wraiths} across the rooms). B34, Malice Aforethought: a murder-haunt (as the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.obsessionHaunt01]{Obsession} pattern, lethal Strikes). B35: the Prison of Woe. B37: the hidden mooring — the Tarnworm's other hunting ground.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.danseMacabre0001]{Danse Macabre (14)}
B36 — the Rosette ObservatoryLow · 60 XP @ L13

The @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.danseMacabre0001]{DANSE MACABRE} — the one horror even the Lords avoid. Nothing here is worth the dance. (The players will go anyway.)

pages.name: C. Second Floor & the Great Hall (C1-C20)

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.5, C — p.292.

  • C1 — Gate-winch: 10 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.scarwallGuard001]{Scarwall guards}.
  • C4 — The Armory: an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.t4bHhZBBDUyVzEmf]{iron golem} (Trivial · 40 XP @ L13) before racks of five-century steel (the castle's best mundane arms + a +2 striking cache).
  • C6/C7: the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.obsessionHaunt01]{Obsession Haunts} (servitude / battle).
  • C8: 4 geists (Elite @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Gu0cJHGwPd547OtC]{wraiths}). C9: a riderless @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.qRUqoezeEnQ2KdyT]{nightmare} stabled in shadow. C10: the torture room's penitent dead.
  • C19/C20 — Anizora: the hunt-commander, a debilitated @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.IrssIkWkW6fsbHJL]{dybbuk} (Weak adjustment) — she possesses a PC and walks them toward the smithy's forge.
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mithrodarChain01]{Mithrodar (16) — the Chained Spirit}
C14 — THE GREAT HALL: MithrodarModerate · 80 XP @ L14

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mithrodarChain01]{MITHRODAR} enthroned in chains. Before all four anchors fall he re-forms in 1d4 days — fighting him early is how the castle teaches. After the fourth: the true battle, every freed soul straining at his chains. (Coordinate the curse-ladder on his sheet.)

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.5, D — p.300.

D1, the Gatehouse Loft: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.castothraneSk001]{CASTOTHRANE} — ANCHOR — and his skeletal garrison (Trivial · 30 XP @ L13). D8, the Minarets: the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gargoyleBrute001]{gargoyle} roosts (2–8 by provocation). D12: a guard-captain post. D14, the Tower of Kazavon: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.nihilDevil000001]{NIHIL} — ANCHOR — in his chains (Low · 60 XP @ L13; talk first — he wants this). D15/D16, Belfry & Cupola: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.aerilayaBanshe01]{Aerilaya} and her court (Low · 60 XP @ L13 with two bone-devil 'wraiths'). D18: the Lord's Overlook — the castle's one honest view.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.5, E — p.308.

E3: 3 elite @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.scarwallGuard001]{guards}. E5: 2 Nessian hell hounds (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.15TyWlbDQWNjMKeL]{greater hell hounds}, Elite). E7: Risibeth the sayona — blood for secrets (her price: a pint, willingly; her goods: three anchors named, the chapel fingered, the castle's history). E10: the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.curseOfTheWorm01]{Curse of the Worm} over the army's records. E13: 4 spectres (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.ZQ7lS9TYOFv07m1a]{war-wraiths}).

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gorstavJuju00001]{Gorstav (13)}
E11 — the War Room: GorstavTrivial · 40 XP @ L13

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.gorstavJuju00001]{Gorstav} at his eternal sand-table, refighting the day he lost. Carriers of Serithtial's name beware.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.5, F — p.314.

F2: a fiendish elder water elemental in the font (Trivial · 20 XP @ L14; run as Elite @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.R427CMT90S7fv7MY]{elemental tsunami}, Weak). F3: 5 advanced spectres (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.ZQ7lS9TYOFv07m1a]{war-wraiths}). F8: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mingorcDonjon001]{Mingorc} at the porter's station (Trivial · 30 XP @ L14). F10: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.prelateAruth0001]{Prelate Aruth} and his congregation (Trivial · 30 XP @ L14).

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.zevRavenkaDemi01]{Zev Ravenka (15) — ANCHOR}
F11 — the Dark Shrine of MidnightLow · 60 XP @ L14

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.zevRavenkaDemi01]{ZEV RAVENKA} — the fourth ANCHOR — a jeweled skull over Zon-Kuthon's altar. One round to wake; then Trap Soul every round. His rubies are the donjon's treasury — and some of them scream.

pages.name: G-H. The Star Tower & the Sacred Lake

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.5, G-H — p.320.

The Star Tower descends, not rises — a well of dark at the castle's root, and below it, water that remembers a sword.

G7, the Chamber of the Well: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ildervokNight001]{ILDERVOK}, invisible and courteous (Trivial · 40 XP @ L14 if it comes to blades). His toll: a curate for Zon-Kuthon's post. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.shadowcountSial1]{Sial} wants it; Sial would prefer Laori got it; Laori refuses, hurt — the Brotherhood's quiet knife-fight, decided by the PCs' word. A banishment-grade effect also clears the post (and angers a god). H1–H2: the caverns (a sealed gate to Sekamina). H3: the Sacred Lake — 4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.QLyDRZPvMsYGZEeN]{gugs} haunt its shore (Trivial · 40 XP @ L14).

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kleestadPharma01]{Kleestad (15)}
H4 — KLEESTAD & SERITHTIALLow · 60 XP @ L14

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kleestadPharma01]{Kleestad}, the traitor-worm, coiled on the lakebed around the sword he can never wield. Serithtial sleeps suppressed (a masterwork blade until the curse lifts): a +3 major-striking holy bastard sword, Kazavon's bane, that chooses a good-hearted bearer — the only edge that can sunder the Crown of Fangs.

Conclusion. Serithtial recovered (and ideally the curse broken — every soul in the walls released, including any the castle took from the party), the PCs return across Belkzen. They must be well into L15, sword in hand, before Castle Korvosa.

A Swifter Scarwall (optional, recommended): the full castle is a long crawl — the AP's own advice: run B1→B5 (Mandraivus), one wing of choice, the four anchors, C14, and the Star Tower descent; compress the rest to random encounters and dread. Interleave Korvosa beats (the Epidemic Clock has been climbing unattended — the persistent-plague overlay's away-penalty) so the city's decay pulls the party home.

Redemption threads: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.shadowcountSial1]{Sial} likely takes the curacy (gone to the Plane of Shadow) or dies; Laori refuses it, survives, and — befriended — may begin the long walk away from Zon-Kuthon. Mithrodar's fall frees Zellara if the castle took her. Kleestad, if he somehow lived, pursues the sword to the world's end. Next: Chapter 6 — Crown of Fangs.
